creator: Christopher.petrik@usm.edu
text:
I have a patch for a new mbestls3 port but the current maintainer of mbestls was not very helpful. Not sure why you would port it back instead of just using the internal one , you are modifying the code when the app comes with its recent internal mbestls3 port at its latest version.

I would trust the internal lib with non modified code rather then hacking it to work for a lts version, very easy to get frustrated when current maintainers don’t want to accept new ports which we would need later on any ways 2.x won’t last forever. Easier to work with the openbsd and netbsd projects in regards to this rather than a whole heap of reviews and edits and oh please resubmit the patch with git so I don’t have to do any leg work. All this makes a simple process overly complex for no reason.
